description: Wikidata P31 class QIDs to pull from SPARQL. Each active entry fetches up to --limit events per pipeline run. Wikipedia category links show the editorial taxonomy that maps to each class. QIDs are validated via scripts/discover-categories.py — run it to check counts and find new candidates.

categories.label: conflicts
categories.notes: Covers battles, wars, sieges, naval battles, civil wars, invasions, rebellions, conquests, military operations

categories.label: coups
categories.notes: Not always a P279* subclass of Q180684 in Wikidata, so needs its own entry

categories.label: assassinations
categories.notes: P301 from Category:Assassinations. Broader than Q1139665 (political murder).

categories.label: social movements
categories.notes: Covers civil rights, suffrage, labor movements, protest campaigns

categories.label: disasters
categories.notes: P301 from Category:Disasters. Broader than Q8065 (natural disaster) — covers industrial, man-made, and natural events.

categories.label: epidemics
categories.notes: P301 from Category:Epidemics. Not always under Q3839081 (disaster) so needs its own entry.

categories.label: famines
categories.notes: P301 from Category:Famines.

categories.label: economic crises
categories.notes: P301 from Category:Economic crises. Covers financial crises, bank panics, recessions, depressions.

categories.label: expeditions
categories.notes: P301 from Category:Expeditions.

categories.label: ecclesiastical councils
categories.notes: Covers councils, synods, church assemblies

categories.label: declarations of independence
categories.notes: Captures national independence declarations — Haiti (1804), USA (1776), etc. Not reliably captured under revolutions or conflicts.

categories.label: founding events
categories.notes: Founding of states, cities, institutions

categories.label: scientific discoveries
categories.notes: Individual discovery events: chemical elements (oxygen, hydrogen, sodium), medical breakthroughs (Jenner's vaccine), physical laws. Many important pre-1850 science events use this P31 type.

categories.label: astronomical discoveries
categories.notes: Discovery events for comets, asteroids, planets, moons. Wikidata items often use this specific class without the broader Q2678658 parent, so listed separately for full coverage. [disabled: P279* traversal returns huge intermediate result, hangs 40+ min]

categories.label: archaeological discoveries
categories.notes: Excavations and artifact finds: Rosetta Stone (1799), Pompeii excavations (ongoing 18th c.), Herculaneum. Distinct from scientific experiments.

categories.label: protests
categories.notes: Demonstrations, marches, rallies. Women's March on Versailles (1789), Boston Tea Party, etc. Not always tagged under Q180684 (conflict) in Wikidata.

categories.label: strikes
categories.notes: Labor strikes, general strikes. Key for labor history from the Industrial Revolution onward.

categories.label: referendums
categories.notes: Independence referendums, constitutional referendums, plebiscites. French Revolutionary-era plebiscites, 19th-century independence votes.

categories.label: international conferences
categories.notes: Diplomatic congresses and summits: Congress of Vienna (1814–15), Peace of Westphalia (1648), Congress of Rastatt (1798), Berlin Conference (1884). [disabled: P279* traversal hangs WDQS]

categories.label: inaugurations
categories.notes: Presidential inaugurations, opening ceremonies for major institutions. US presidential inaugurations (Washington 1789, Adams, Jefferson, etc.) are well-tagged.

categories.label: annexations
categories.notes: Territorial annexations: Louisiana Purchase-era acquisitions, Napoleonic annexations of Italian states, etc.

categories.label: canonizations
categories.notes: Catholic Church canonizations. Well-catalogued in Wikidata with dates and locations.

categories.label: beatifications
categories.notes: Catholic Church beatifications (penultimate step before canonization). Distinct class from canonization in Wikidata.

categories.label: schisms
categories.notes: Religious splits: Great Schism (1054), Protestant Reformation schisms, Old Catholic splits, Eastern Orthodox schisms.

categories.label: religious persecutions
categories.notes: Inquisitions, pogroms, mass expulsions based on religion. Already partially covered by Q3464753 in extract.py but this fetches from SPARQL.

categories.label: pilgrimages
categories.notes: Major historical pilgrimages: Hajj milestones, Canterbury, Santiago de Compostela routes. Bridges religion and culture. [disabled: 108k QIDs mostly Hajj performances, takes hours to download]

categories.label: art exhibitions
categories.notes: Gallery and museum exhibitions: Royal Academy of Arts annual shows, Paris Salon, Impressionist exhibitions. [Fixed: was Q464980=exhibition (too broad). Correct QID Q667276=art exhibition. Direct P31 returns 451 items.]

categories.label: world's fairs
categories.notes: International expositions: Crystal Palace (1851), Paris Exposition Universelle, Chicago World's Fair, Expo 67. First major fair 1851 so no results before that. [Fixed: was using Q188686=military occupation by mistake. Correct QID is Q172754=world's fair]

categories.label: olympic games
categories.notes: Modern Summer Olympics (1896+). Each edition is a separate Wikidata item. [Fixed: was Q5389=Olympic Games (abstract concept, 0 direct P31 hits). Use Q135976384=Summer Olympic Games edition. Winter Olympics use Q137592217 but can add separately.]

categories.label: discoveries
categories.notes: SKIP: Only 12 dated Wikipedia results via P31/P279*. Wikipedia's 'Category:Discoveries' contains meta-articles (lists, timelines), not individual discovery events. Geographic discoveries come through via expeditions (Q2401485); scientific ones via scientific experiments (Q11862829). Run --discover on subcategories like Category:Archaeological discoveries to find better QIDs.

categories.label: inventions
categories.notes: SKIP: Q4026292 causes SPARQL timeout (class hierarchy too deep). Individual inventions in Wikidata use highly varied P31 types. Run --discover on Category:Inventions subcategories to find tractable sub-classes.

categories.label: trials
categories.notes: SKIP: Wikidata P31 data for historical trials is inconsistent — Nuremberg has no P31, Galileo='dyad', Socrates='commune of France'. No reliable class QID. Famous trials come through via Q180684 (conflicts) or manual entry.

categories.label: crusades
categories.notes: SKIP: Q8441 (former QID for 'crusade') was reassigned to 'man' in Wikidata. Q12546 (Crusades series) returns only 1 Wikipedia result. Individual crusades are captured via Q180684 (conflicts).
